spec.title: synthkit · Self-Observability
spec.description: Internal self-monitoring of the synthkit generator process (service.name=synthkit) on the staff stack — push-pipeline RED, per-construct-instance tick health, Go runtime, Pyroscope profiling (conditional tab), self-obs traces/spanmetrics, and the operational log stream. Source: internal/selfobs + internal/profiling.

spec.title: Telemetry freshness
spec.description: Seconds since the generator's self-obs metrics last arrived in staff stack (15s emit interval). Green < 2 min. Red ⇒ the synthkit process is down or its OTLP export to staff stack is broken.

spec.title: Goroutines
spec.description: Live goroutine count. A steady climb = goroutine leak.

spec.title: Memory in use
spec.description: Total Go runtime memory in use (stack + other), summed.

spec.title: Request-ledger size
spec.description: Current total request-ledger size across all blueprints (entries retained for join). Unbounded growth = ledger not being trimmed.

spec.title: Push attempts/s
spec.description: Synthetic-data push attempts per second across all sinks.

spec.title: Push error ratio
spec.description: Fraction of push attempts that failed (any non-ok, non-dry_run outcome). Healthy ≈ 0. Rising ⇒ a sink endpoint is rejecting (429/4xx/5xx) — see the Push tab.

spec.title: Tick error ratio
spec.description: Fraction of construct/workload instance ticks that returned an error.

spec.title: Blueprints loaded
spec.description: Number of blueprints currently loaded and ticking. Matches the blueprints/ directory the generator was started with.

spec.title: Queue depth (max)
spec.description: Maximum enqueued-but-unflushed items across all delivery queue shards/sinks. Healthy ≈ 0. Sustained > 0 means delivery is falling behind generation.

spec.title: Backpressure events/s
spec.description: Tick-enqueue blocks/s across all sinks — a tick tried to enqueue but a queue shard was full. Healthy = 0. Any non-zero value means the host can't ship as fast as it generates.

spec.title: Push attempts/s by outcome
spec.description: Stacked push rate split by outcome. ok / error / rate_limited / client_error / server_error / dry_run.

spec.title: Push attempts/s by sink
spec.description: Push rate per sink (faro / loki / otlp / promrw).

spec.title: Tick rate by outcome
spec.description: Construct/workload instance tick invocations/s by outcome.

spec.title: Recent warnings & errors
spec.description: Live tail of the generator's WARN/ERROR log lines from staff stack Loki. Empty = healthy.

spec.title: Total push rate
spec.description: Push attempts/s per sink.

spec.title: Push errors/s by sink & outcome
spec.description: Failed push attempts/s, split by sink and failure outcome.

spec.expr: sum by (sink, outcome) (rate(synthkit_push_total{job="synthkit", instance=~"$instance", outcome!="ok", outcome!="dry_run"}[$__rate_interval]))

spec.title: Push attempts/s by blueprint
spec.description: Push rate attributed to each blueprint. The `blueprint` label is stamped on blueprint-scoped pushes; substrate-scoped pushes (k8s, dbo11y, CSP …) carry no blueprint and group under the empty series — that omission is by design (an absent dimension is never "").

spec.title: Average push latency by sink
spec.description: Mean wall-clock per push, per sink (rate of _sum ÷ rate of _count). NOTE: the duration histogram uses OTel default buckets (smallest 5s) but real pushes are sub-second, so they all fall in one bucket and histogram_quantile is meaningless — the mean is the accurate measure. For true span quantiles see the Traces tab (Tempo native histogram).

spec.title: Items pushed/s by sink
spec.description: Logical items (series / log lines / spans / RUM beacons) pushed per second.

spec.title: Wire bytes pushed/s by sink
spec.description: Wire bytes/s (sinks that build their own body — faro / loki / otlp; promrw is excluded by design ⇒ no series).

spec.title: Push items/s by blueprint
spec.description: Logical items (series / log lines / spans / RUM beacons) pushed per second, attributed to each blueprint. The `blueprint` label is omitted for substrate-scoped pushes (k8s, dbo11y, CSP …) — an absent dimension is never "".

spec.title: Push bytes/s by blueprint
spec.description: Wire bytes/s attributed to each blueprint (faro / loki / otlp; promrw excluded by design). The `blueprint` label is omitted for substrate-scoped pushes — an absent dimension is never "".

spec.title: Push throughput by signal type
spec.description: Logical items/s grouped by telemetry signal type (metrics / traces / logs / rum / profiles), derived deterministically from the sink. Complements the per-sink view — the generator's output mix by signal class. NEW: requires a build emitting the signal_type label on synthkit_push_items_total.

spec.title: Flush latency by sink (p50/p95)
spec.description: Per-flush wall-clock of one delivery-queue batch ship, by sink. Uses FINE explicit histogram buckets (.005–10s), so quantiles ARE accurate here (unlike the coarse default-bucket push/cycle duration histograms). Rising p95 = a sink endpoint is slow — correlate with queue depth + backpressure. NEW.

spec.title: Flush batch size by sink (avg)
spec.description: Mean items shipped per flush, by sink. Small (→1) = deadline-driven flushes (partial batches, the queue is keeping up); near BatchMax = capacity-driven (throughput-bound). A sustained climb toward BatchMax with rising depth means delivery is falling behind generation. NEW.

spec.title: Flush error rate by sink
spec.description: Failed delivery-queue flushes/s by sink — the sink's flush() returned an error after its own retries, so a whole batch ship failed on the background sender. Healthy = 0. Complementary to the push-outcome RED (per-attempt): this is per-batch-ship on the decoupled queue. NEW.

spec.title: Queue depth by sink
spec.description: Enqueued-but-unflushed items per sink delivery queue. Healthy ≈ 0. Sustained growth = delivery falling behind generation — raise the tick interval or lower the volume multiplier, or investigate sink latency on the Push tab.

spec.title: Enqueue-blocked rate by sink
spec.description: Tick-enqueue blocks/s per sink — increments each time a tick blocked trying to enqueue because a queue shard was full (backpressure). Healthy = 0. Any non-zero value means the host can't ship as fast as it generates for that sink.

spec.title: Queue depth (max, now)
spec.description: Maximum queue depth across all sinks right now. Healthy ≈ 0.

spec.title: Backpressure events/s (now)
spec.description: Total enqueue-blocked events/s across all sinks. Healthy = 0.

spec.title: $sink · push rate
spec.description: Push attempts/s for this sink.

spec.title: $sink · error %
spec.description: Error ratio for this sink.

spec.expr: (sum(rate(synthkit_push_total{job="synthkit", instance=~"$instance", sink=~"$sink", outcome!="ok", outcome!="dry_run"}[$__rate_interval])) or vector(0)) / clamp_min(sum(rate(synthkit_push_total{job="synthkit", instance=~"$instance", sink=~"$sink"}[$__rate_interval])), 0.0001)

spec.title: $sink · avg latency
spec.description: Mean push wall-clock for this sink (the duration histogram's default buckets are too coarse for quantiles — see the summary-panel note).

spec.title: $sink · avg latency
spec.description: Mean push latency for this sink over time.

spec.title: $sink · items/s & bytes/s
spec.description: Throughput for this sink. faro / loki / otlp report bytes (promrw is excluded by design ⇒ no bytes series).

spec.title: Dropped ticks (window)
spec.description: Total ticks dropped over the selected window (summed across blueprints). A drop = the prior cycle for that blueprint was still running when the next tick fired, so the runner skipped it. Healthy = 0. > 0 ⇒ the generator is not keeping up — raise the tick interval or lower the volume multiplier. Correlate with the cycle-duration panel.

spec.title: Slowest avg cycle
spec.description: Mean full-cycle wall-clock of the slowest blueprint. Approaching the configured tick interval is the warning sign that precedes dropped ticks.

spec.title: Avg cycle duration by blueprint
spec.description: Mean wall-clock of one full blueprint cycle (rate of _sum ÷ rate of _count). NOTE: the cycle-duration histogram uses OTel default buckets, too coarse for quantiles — the mean is the accurate measure (same as the push/tick duration panels). Watch this trend toward the tick interval.

spec.title: Dropped ticks/s by blueprint
spec.description: Dropped ticks per second, per blueprint. Any non-zero series ⇒ that blueprint's cycle is overrunning its tick interval. Empty = every cycle finished in time (healthy).

spec.title: Per-instance health
spec.description: One row per construct/workload instance: kind, blueprint, tick rate, total errors in the window, and mean tick duration. Sort by Errors to find the offender. NOTE: requires a generator build with the selfobs `construct_instance` rename — empty on older builds.

spec.expr: sum by (construct_instance, construct_kind, blueprint) (increase(synthkit_tick_total{job="synthkit", instance=~"$instance", outcome="error"}[$__range]))

sum by (construct_instance, construct_kind, blueprint) (rate(synthkit_tick_duration_seconds_sum{job="synthkit", instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum by (construct_instance, construct_kind, blueprint) (rate(synthkit_tick_duration_seconds_count{job="synthkit", instance=~"$instance"}[$__rate_interval]))

spec.title: Tick rate by instance (top 15)
spec.description: Tick invocations/s, top 15 instances by rate.

spec.title: Tick errors by instance (in window)
spec.description: Total tick errors per instance over the selected window, worst first. Empty = all instances healthy.

spec.title: Avg tick duration by instance (top 15)
spec.description: Mean wall-clock per tick, top 15 slowest instances (histogram buckets too coarse for quantiles).

spec.title: Tick error rate by instance
spec.description: Per-instance tick error rate over time (only erroring instances shown).

spec.title: Tick rate/s by construct kind
spec.description: Tick invocations per second, summed by construct kind. Shows which construct families are generating the most tick activity across all blueprints.

spec.title: Tick rate/s by blueprint
spec.description: Tick invocations per second, summed by blueprint. The `blueprint` label is omitted for substrate-scoped constructs (k8s, dbo11y, CSP …) — an absent dimension is never "".

spec.title: Total distinct series
spec.description: Total distinct synthetic series currently emitted across all constructs/workloads and blueprints. This is an X-ray high-water count from the internal bookkeeping; it reflects the current cardinality footprint synthkit puts on the target stack.

spec.title: Distinct series by blueprint
spec.description: Distinct synthetic series broken down by blueprint. Shows the relative cardinality contribution of each blueprint; substrate-scoped constructs (k8s, dbo11y, CSP …) group under the empty series — an absent blueprint dimension is never "".

spec.title: Top constructs by cardinality
spec.description: Top 15 construct/workload instances by distinct series count. Use this to identify which instances contribute the most to the target stack's cardinality.

spec.title: Cardinality by construct kind
spec.description: Distinct synthetic series summed by construct kind. Reveals which construct families (e.g. k8s_cluster, ec2, rds, app …) carry the highest cardinality footprint.

spec.title: Goroutines
spec.description: Live goroutines. Sustained growth = leak.

spec.title: Memory in use by type + GC goal
spec.description: Go memory in use (stack / other) and the GC heap-size goal. Used approaching goal ⇒ GC about to run.

spec.title: Allocation rate (bytes/s)
spec.description: Heap bytes allocated per second (cumulative counter rate). Allocation pressure driving GC.

spec.title: Allocation rate (objects/s)
spec.description: Heap object allocations per second.

spec.title: GOMAXPROCS
spec.description: go_processor_limit — CPUs the runtime may use.

spec.title: GOGC
spec.description: go_config_gogc_percent — GC target percentage (default 100).

spec.title: Goroutines (now)
spec.description: Current goroutine count.

spec.title: Memory in use (now)
spec.description: Current total memory in use.

spec.title: CPU profile (time)
spec.description: CPU nanoseconds attributed per step from the continuous CPU profile (Pyroscope).

spec.title: In-use heap (profile)
spec.description: Live heap in use, from the inuse_space profile. Cross-check against go_memory_used_bytes on the Go runtime tab.

spec.title: Alloc space (profile)
spec.description: Bytes allocated per step from the alloc_space profile — allocation pressure (drives GC).

spec.title: Goroutines (profile)
spec.description: Goroutine count from the goroutine profile. Should track go_goroutine_count on the Go runtime tab.

spec.title: CPU flame graph
spec.description: Where CPU time is spent across the selected window (merged CPU profile). Widen the time range for a more representative graph.

spec.title: In-use space flame graph
spec.description: Call stacks holding live heap (inuse_space) — where retained memory is allocated.

spec.title: Alloc space flame graph
spec.description: Call stacks driving allocations (alloc_space) — the GC/allocation hot paths.

spec.title: Goroutines flame graph
spec.description: Call stacks of live goroutines — useful for spotting goroutine leaks (which package is parked where).

spec.title: Mutex contention
spec.description: Lock-contention wait time (delay, ns) and contention count from the mutex profile. Sustained growth points at a hot lock serialising the cycle — confirm location in the flame graph.

spec.title: Goroutine block
spec.description: Time goroutines spent blocked (delay, ns) and block count from the block profile — channel/select, mutex, and network/IO waits. High block delay with low CPU = the cycle is waiting, not computing.

spec.title: Alloc objects
spec.description: Heap objects allocated (count) from the alloc_objects profile — allocation churn by object count, complementary to alloc_space (bytes). High count with modest bytes = many tiny allocations.

spec.title: In-use objects
spec.description: Live heap objects (count) from the inuse_objects profile — object-count footprint, complementary to inuse_space (bytes). Unbounded growth = an object leak (cross-check goroutines + ledger size).

spec.title: Mutex contention flame graph
spec.description: Call stacks where goroutines wait on locks (mutex delay) — pinpoints the contended lock.

spec.title: Goroutine block flame graph
spec.description: Call stacks where goroutines block (block delay) — channel/select/IO waits serialising work.

spec.title: Alloc objects flame graph
spec.description: Call stacks driving object allocations (by count) — the churn hot paths.

spec.title: In-use objects flame graph
spec.description: Call stacks holding live objects (by count) — where retained objects are allocated.

spec.title: Span calls/s by span (cycle · flush · push · fleet)
spec.description: Spanmetrics call rate per span name, derived by Tempo from the self-obs traces. The metered spans are now the operational workflow: `cycle` (per-blueprint generation, CONSUMER), `flush <sink>` (delivery-queue batch ship — how the QUEUED sinks promrw/loki/otlp/pyroscope become visible, since the decoupled queue ships them off the traced tick path), `push faro` (synchronous RUM push, CLIENT) and `fleet <op>` (FM register/heartbeat, CLIENT). The tick root stays INTERNAL ⇒ not metered.

spec.title: Span error rate by span
spec.description: Errored spans/s per span name (status_code=ERROR). Empty = no failing pushes.

spec.title: Span latency p50/p95/p99 (all spans, blended)
spec.description: True span-duration quantiles from traces_spanmetrics_latency — a Tempo-generated NATIVE histogram (seconds), so quantiles are accurate here (unlike the coarse push-duration histogram on the Push tab).

spec.title: p95 span latency by span
spec.description: p95 latency per span name (push faro / loki / otlp / promrw), from the Tempo native histogram.

spec.title: Recent tick traces
spec.description: Most recent self-obs traces (root span tick, with push <sink> children). Click a trace ID to open it in Tempo. Use the time picker to widen the window.

spec.title: Log volume by severity
spec.description: Log lines per interval by severity, inferred from line content by the self-obs log bridge. severity_text is structured metadata (filtered via the | pipe); one explicit query per level since Loki does not propagate structured-metadata labels through sum by(). Drawn as stacked BARS: WARN/ERROR are sparse (often 1–2 lines/min), so bars stay legible and isolating a single severity in the legend renders its (scattered) bars rather than looking empty as stacked lines did.

spec.title: Error log rate
spec.description: Error log lines per second. > 0 ⇒ check the error stream below.

spec.title: Warn log rate
spec.description: Warning log lines per second.

spec.title: Error stream
spec.description: ERROR-level lines only. Empty = healthy.

spec.title: Runner tick errors/s by blueprint · unit
spec.description: Per-construct/workload tick errors the runner logged, parsed from free text at query time (blueprint + unit extracted via LogQL regexp). Complements the structured Push-failures panel: that is sink-level push errors, this is every runner-level tick error (construct logic, workload, master tick) broken down by blueprint·unit. Master-tick lines show with an empty unit.

spec.expr: sum by (blueprint, unit) (count_over_time({service_name="synthkit"} |= "runner: blueprint" | regexp "runner: blueprint \"(?P<blueprint>[^\"]+)\" (?:(?P<kind>construct|workload) \"(?P<unit>[^\"]+)\"|(?P<kind2>master tick)):" [$__auto]))

spec.title: Push failures (structured)
spec.description: Failed synthetic pushes as STRUCTURED logs (event=push_error) from selfobs.PushObserver — the only place a push error's reason reaches the log stream (otherwise it lives only in the metric + trace span). Structured metadata carries sink / blueprint / outcome / http.status_code, so you can filter to one sink or blueprint (expand a line → Fields). rate_limited is WARN; other failures are ERROR. Substrate-scoped pushes carry no blueprint (omitted by design). Empty = no push failures.

spec.title: Runner tick errors
spec.description: The runner's per-tick error lines, with blueprint / kind / unit parsed into fields (expand a line → Fields to filter by blueprint or construct). This is the panel to watch for tick-level failures, broken down by the construct/workload that failed. Empty = no construct/workload/master-tick errors.

spec.title: Full operational stream
spec.description: All self-obs log lines (scope synthkit/selfobs). Includes startup, heartbeat, per-push info (dry-run summaries when DRY_RUN=true), and errors.

spec.title: Build / identity
spec.description: Resource identity of the running generator: version, env tag, instance, telemetry_kind.

spec.title: Volume multiplier
spec.description: Control-plane master volume multiplier — scales synthetic output across all blueprints. Driven live from the operator UI / control-state.json.

spec.title: Blueprints loaded
spec.description: Number of loaded blueprints.

spec.title: Request-ledger size
spec.description: Total request-ledger size across all blueprints (now).

spec.title: Volume multiplier over time
spec.description: Live master volume multiplier over time — steps mark a re-configuration of the generator's load shape.

spec.title: Request-ledger size over time
spec.description: Correlation request-ledger size. Should plateau; unbounded growth = trim not running.

options.content:
## synthkit · Self-Observability

Internal monitoring of the **synthkit generator process itself** (`service.name=synthkit`), instrumented by `internal/selfobs` + `internal/profiling` and shipped to the **staff** stack over OTLP/Pyroscope (creds `GC_SELF_OTLP_*` / `GC_PYROSCOPE_*`) — wholly separate from the synthetic telemetry synthkit produces for the target stack.

| Signal | Source |
|---|---|
| `synthkit_push_*` | RED on the synthetic-push pipeline (by sink / **signal_type** / blueprint / outcome) |
| `synthkit_queue_flush_*` | per-flush latency (fine buckets) / batch size / flush-error count by sink — **Push** tab |
| `event="config_change"` | control-plane mutation audit (volume / failures / enablement) — **Config** tab |
| `target_info{run_mode}` | live vs dry_run process identity (dashboards exclude dry_run) |
| `synthkit_tick_*` | per construct/workload instance tick health (`construct_instance`, `construct_kind`, `blueprint`) |
| `synthkit_cardinality_series` | distinct synthetic series per construct/workload instance (X-ray cardinality bookkeeping) — **Cardinality** tab |
| `synthkit_cycle_duration_seconds`, `synthkit_dropped_ticks_total` | per-blueprint cycle pacing — is the generator keeping up? (`blueprint`) |
| `synthkit_queue_depth` | enqueued-but-unflushed items per sink delivery queue (`sink`); gauge; healthy ≈ 0 |
| `synthkit_queue_enqueue_blocked_total` | tick-enqueue blocks/s per sink — backpressure counter; healthy = 0 |
| `synthkit_ledger_size`, `synthkit_volume_multiplier`, `synthkit_blueprint_count` | live process state (observable gauges) |
| `go_*` | Go runtime (OTel runtime instrumentation) |
| `traces_spanmetrics_*` | Tempo-derived RED on the `push <sink>` spans |
| Loki `{service_name="synthkit"}` | operational log stream (scope `synthkit/selfobs`) |
| Loki `event="push_error"` | structured push failures (sink/blueprint/outcome/status) — **Logs** tab |
| Tempo `tick` | per-tick traces, `push <sink>` children |
| Pyroscope `service_name="synthkit"` | continuous profiles (`internal/profiling`) — **Profiling** tab |

The **Profiling** tab is *conditionally rendered*: it appears only when Pyroscope profiles are present (`PYROSCOPE_ENABLED=true` and shipping to staff stack); with profiling off it hides.

**Note:** the **Cycle & instance health** tab needs a generator build carrying the selfobs `construct_instance` label (the legacy `instance` datapoint attribute collided with the Prometheus target `instance` label and was dropped); it reads empty on older builds.

The **Cardinality** tab uses `synthkit_cardinality_series{blueprint, construct_kind, construct_instance}` — a gauge tracking the distinct synthetic series each instance has ever emitted. The `blueprint` label is omitted for substrate-scoped constructs (absent dimension ≠ "").

spec.title: Config-change audit (control-plane mutations)
spec.description: Operator control-plane mutations as STRUCTURED logs (event=config_change) from selfobs.EmitEvent — every successful POST to /control/* (volume / failure injection / blueprint+construct+kind enablement / scenarios / scaling) emits one line carrying the new knob state. Structured metadata: volume_multiplier, failures_active, disabled_blueprints/constructs/kinds, active_scenarios, span_metrics_bps (expand a line → Fields). Empty = no control changes in the window. NEW.
